title: populus × canadensis f. erecta
intro: Populus × canadensis f. erecta, also known as the black cottonwood or black poplar, is a hybrid plant species between the eastern cottonwood (Populus deltoides) and the balsam poplar (Populus balsamifera). This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, making it effective in reducing pain and inflammation. The health benefits of Populus × canadensis f. erecta include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic and antispasmodic. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, which are used to alleviate various ailments and promote overall well-being.
